perf.regime: CUTE DSL fp32 weighted top-k reduction on Grace+Blackwell GB10 (sm_121). Memory-bound: one CTA per token row m, 128 threads tile the hidden dim H (thread-stride, coalesced). The reduction axis (top_k=8) is tiny, so each thread holds its output element and sums top_k weighted y values in fp32 (Kahan) — no block-wide reduction, no SMEM, no sync. Host upcasts y/w to fp32 (bit-identical to the reference's y.float()) and casts back to y.dtype, matching the triton card's fp32-multiply-then-cast order.

measured.source: scripts/archive/ds5-probes/ds5_moe_sum_perf.py + ncu on ds5 GB10 sm_121, CTK 13.0, post compile-cache. END-TO-END incl launch.

notes:
CUTE DSL (`cutlass.cute`, nvidia-cutlass-dsl 4.5.2) backend for moe_sum_reduce; third native-CUDA card in the library, validated on the ds5 Grace+Blackwell GB10 test bed (docs/usage/ds5-testbed.md). JIT-compiles via nvcc (CUDA_HOME must be set). Registers only if nvidia-cutlass-dsl imports; else degrades to the triton/reference card.

Design (src/xkernels/ops/moe/cute/sum_reduce_kernel.py): the reduction axis (top_k=8 in the sweep) is TINY, so this is a per-thread sum over k, NOT a block-wide reduction (unlike dual_rmsnorm). One CTA per token row; 128 threads tile H; each thread sums top_k weighted y values in fp32 (Kahan) and scales by routed_scaling_factor. Pure-fp32 device kernel (host does bf16<->fp32 plumbing).

Compile-cache: per-(M,H) cute.compile handle (tensors-only launch), same pattern as mm_fp8_blockscale.cuda. Device guard raises RuntimeError on CPU tensors so verify_parity (device='cpu') records CUDA as a caught backend error instead of segfaulting.
